source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Consumer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




THE MISSION

At Counsel, we believe everyone should have an expert physician in their pocket. We have built the foundation—medical record integrations, doctor-led AI, and immediate clinical access. Now, we need a Senior Senior Product Manager, Consumer to turn that foundation into a daily habit for millions.

You will own the "Day 0 to Day 30" experience, ensuring every user who discovers Counsel doesn't just "try" it, but realizes it is the only way they want to manage their health. You’ll also own our onboarding flows and core chat experience - leveraging our differentiators of clinicians on the platform and doctors-in-the-loop to solve user needs.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Engineer the "Aha!" Moment: You will obsess over time-to-value. Whether it’s getting a user to sync their first record or have a user interact with a doctor, you will design the path that makes our value undeniable.

 - Master Mindshare & Presence: You will define what "presence" looks like for Counsel. Through notification strategies, mobile app adoption, and habit-forming triggers, you will drive users to return to Counsel as their first instinct for any care issue.

 - Acquisition funnel: You will own the "front door" of Counsel. This means treating the signed-out experience, landing pages, and the onboarding flow as a core product that converts curiosity into high-intent usage.

 - Core chat experience: This is the actual modality of how users get care today! You’ll be able to work closely with our AI teams to improve the quality and personalization of conversations users have with AI and our doctor.

 - Industry Agnostic: We don’t care if you’ve worked in health tech. We care that you’ve grown a consumer product at scale.

 - Partner with D2C Growth marketing: You will work in a high-velocity pod alongside our D2C Growth Marketer.




WHO YOU ARE / WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - You have 4+ years of experience in building consumer products.

 - You have done 0→1→10 work before.

 - Lead discovery and experimentation to identify and prioritize the most impactful features and opportunities

 - Deep customer intuition and product taste, combining design empathy with a rigorous, data-informed approach to measuring impact

 - Ability to own your work in a fast-paced, rapid growth environment, manage multiple priorities, and adapt to an ever-changing landscape

 - Ability to roll up your sleeves and do whatever it takes to make your product successful.

 - Work cross functionally with engineering, design, clinicians, and GTM to define, build, and deploy experiences that delight our users

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills




BONUS POINTS FOR

 - Doing growth at an early-stage startup during its primary scaling phase

 - Direct experience working with LLM-powered products.

 - Prior work collaborating with D2C growth marketing teams or being a growth marketer you

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




ABOUT THE ROLE

At Counsel, we've built a product that patients and physicians love, on a fast-moving stack. As we scale, the line between "application code" and "infrastructure" keeps blurring and we need someone who lives right at that intersection.

We're looking for a Backend Infrastructure Engineer who's equal parts software engineer and infrastructure engineer. You won't be sitting in a silo writing Terraform all day, and you won't be shipping product features in React. You'll own the layer in between: the CI/CD pipelines, the IaC that provisions our AWS environment, the backend services that glue our systems together, and the developer tooling that keeps a fast-growing engineering team moving.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Shape our infrastructure-as-code. Develop and maintain our cloud infrastructure using Terraform.

 - Build and improve CI/CD. Own our build, test, and deployment pipelines end to end. Your goal is to make deploying to production boring; fast, reliable, automatic.

 - Write real backend code. Contribute production backend services and core server-side systems. You write application code, rather than simply orchestrating it.

 - Build our core archetypes. Create production-grade patterns for new deployments, jobs, consumers, and workers. Think: an abstract base stream consumer or cron executor built for reuse across the team.

 - Make other engineers faster. Improve local dev workflows, cut build times, kill flaky infrastructure, and build the internal tools people actually want to use.

 - Strengthen security and compliance. Work closely with our security and compliance team to harden our infrastructure posture — secrets management, IAM policies, network isolation, and audit logging in a HIPAA-regulated environment.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Just as comfortable in an IDE writing application code as in a terminal managing cloud infrastructure, because backend and infra aren't separate disciplines to you.

 - Already scripting the fix by the third time you'd otherwise have to do something manually.

 - Understands how a request travels from a user's browser through a load balancer, into a container, to a database, and back, and can debug any layer along the way.

 - Reaches for the right tool over the trendy one and would rather ship something working today than architect something perfect next quarter.

 - Can explain infrastructure decisions to product engineers and translate product requirements into infrastructure changes.
   
    


WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 3–5 years of professional experience in backend engineering, infrastructure engineering, or a mix of both.

 - Strong proficiency in at least one strongly-typed or compiled language (Go, Rust, Java, Typed Python, TypeScript, etc.).

 - Experience with AWS, GCP, or Azure cloud services.

 - Familiarity with containerized environments and Docker.




BONUS POINTS FOR

 - Hands

source_jobs.title: Director of Strategic Accounts
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




ABOUT THE ROLE

Counsel Health is seeking a Director of Strategic Accounts to drive new business development and growth across our most critical enterprise relationships, including health plans and strategic partners. This role is ideal for a senior, consultative sales/client success operator with experience navigating complex multi-stakeholder healthcare buying processes in an early-stage environment.

You will own deals end-to-end, partnering closely with the SVP, Commercial to support early customer relationships and high-priority B2B sales opportunities. This role spans the full pre- and post-sales lifecycle and is expected to develop deep fluency across deal execution, account performance, and expansion strategy.

As a data-driven sales leader, you are responsible for all account-level reporting, analytics, and insight generation; translating customer performance, market signals, and operational data into clear, actionable recommendations on commercial strategy and long-term growth.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Identify, develop, and close new business opportunities with enterprise healthcare buyers as well as contribute to go-to-market strategy, pricing discussions and sales enablement materials.

 - Manage complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles involving clinical, technical, legal, and executive teams.

 - Build strong relationships with decision makers across health plans and digital health partners, servicing as the primary point of contact pre and post sale.

 - Prepare and synthesize reporting and insights for the SVP, Commercial, supporting pipeline, deal progress, and performance across new and existing accounts.

 - Support onboarding, expansion opportunities, and ongoing relationship management, including gathering customer and market needs to help inform product roadmap and features.

 - Ensure client satisfaction and alignment while expanding Counsel’s relationship to multiple stakeholders.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 7+ years of professional experience; ideal candidate will have 2+ years in an analytical professional services role in consulting, banking/PE and 2+ years in a business development and account management role.

 - Experience in healthcare, digital health, health tech, or related consumer technology industries is highly preferred.

 - Expertise in both the pre-sales and post-sales process, executing contracts and expanding Counsel’s footprint in large complex healthcare and consumer health organizations.

 - Ability to navigate complex multi-stakeholder ecosystems, building consensus and providing innovative solutions.

 - Motivation to build and navigate ambiguity; you are highly energized working in a fast-paced, early-stage company with evolving processes.


LOCATION FOR THIS ROLE:

NYC - hybrid, Boston - hybrid, East Coast - remote




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Impact: Be at the forefront and contribute to groundbreaking,

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer (Frontend)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




ABOUT THE ROLE

Most healthcare still assumes you can walk into an office during business hours. That's left 100 million people without a primary care doctor, turning to Google with a billion medical questions a day instead of a person who can actually help. We're building something better: the new front door to primary care. AI-augmented, physician-supervised, and available 24/7.

We pair patients with in-house doctors through a messaging-first experience and give those doctors an AI clinician cockpit that makes them exponentially more effective. The result is a growing patient base and embedded partnerships with leading digital healthcare enterprises (OURA https://www.linkedin.com/posts/withoura-ouraring5-ugcPost-7465596664364318720-NmbW/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop&rcm=ACoAAAEiCukB-Jg1EMa6h-peEBW_v5gjWlKzQ0s + more TBA).

This is a high-ownership role right at the center of that build. You'll shape the product experiences patients and physicians rely on every day.

This role is hybrid, located in NYC or Boston.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Craft user interfaces for new paradigms of AI-enabled care that improve patient engagement and outcomes

 - Co-create and own product experiences across our patient app and physician-facing care delivery platform, keeping them polished, performant, and accessible

 - Shape frontend architecture, from design systems and state management to responsiveness, testing, and web performance

 - Partner with our ML research and backend teams to bring cutting-edge AI/LLM techniques into clinical workflows and turn them into real product experiences

 - Work closely with Product, Clinical, and Design, as well as prominent healthcare organizations nationwide




WHO YOU ARE

 - Believes great engineering and great design are the same conversation

 - Takes ownership of hard problems and broad initiatives in a high-growth environment, driving them to completion without needing every detail figured out in advance

 - Thrives in collaborative, fast-moving environments where the work is ambitious and the team is exceptional

 - Excited about working at the frontier of medical AI, and what it means to do that responsibly




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 5+ years building production frontend software, startup or high-growth experience is a plus

 - Deep fluency in TypeScript, JavaScript, and CSS, with strong command of React and Next.js for managing complex state and component architecture (bonus points for Node.js or React Native)

 - A sharp eye for detail and real expertise in CSS, animation, and design systems, with a habit of shipping beautiful, high-performing interfaces

 - Familiarity with AI/ML and how recent developments in LLMs apply to real-world product challenges

 - A genuine interest in leveling up your own skills and the team around you
   
   


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Impact: Be at the forefront of AI-augme

source_jobs.title: Physician, Virtual Care (Full-Time, IMLC Required)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




ABOUT THE ROLE

Location: Remote (United States), IMLC LOQ is required to be considered for this role
Team: Clinical
Reports to: VP of Care Delivery
Schedule: Flexible, 0.8 / 0.9 / 1.0 FTE, 32+ hrs/week
Employment: W2

As a Physician on our Virtual Care team, you'll spend the vast majority of your time seeing patients. The difference is how, visits will be fully asynchronous messaging-based, AI-paired via our in-house custom built EHR. You'll be the clinician on the other end of the message: the one a patient turns to at 7pm on a Tuesday for their rash, their refill, or for help managing a chronic illness.

Your day is patient care and every conversation you have with a Counsel patient sharpens how we care for everyone who comes next. That's what it means to multiply the world's healthcare capacity.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Practice exceptional, AI-paired medicine.

 - See patients across acute, primary, and longevity care over messages: diagnose, treat, triage, follow up

 - Practice with Counsel's AI like a sharp colleague who's already read the chart: it preps the history, sharpens the differential, drafts the message. The part only you can do, your judgment, the human on the other end of the message, gets your full attention

 - Hold a high bar on every encounter: the clinical reasoning, the safety, the way it lands with the patient

 - Stay generalist. That means solving in-scope clinical issues yourself and navigating patients to the next-best place in the healthcare system when necessary

Raise the bar on the clinical team.

 - Work with the CMO and VP of Care Delivery on the guidelines, peer review, and clinical systems that keep care safe as the team grows

 - Mentor newer clinicians; share what's working; raise the bar without making it about you

Shape the tools you practice in.

 - Counsel is built on a simple principle: clinicians first, technologists second. The people designing what you practice in want your pushback, your instincts, your taste

 - A workflow that frustrates you this week can be fixed in the next few. A tool you've been waiting years for can land in your hands in a matter of months


WHAT YOU BRING

 - Board certification in Emergency Medicine, Internal Medicine, or Family Medicine and be a member of the Interstate Medical Licensure Compact (IMLC) and hold an IMLC Letter of Qualification

 - 3+ years of post-residency clinical experience (urgent care, ER, primary care, or a strong generalist mix)

 - Genuine interest in acute, primary, and longevity care

 - Comfort working in a fully virtual, asynchronous, messaging-based modality

 - 32+ hrs/week with flexibility on how you build your schedule. Shift blocks (EST): 7:30a–1p, 1p–6p, 6p–9p. Limited evening and weekend coverage required (2 evenings + 2 weekend days/month). 0.8, 0.9, and 1.0 FTE available; W2

 - Strong written communication, most of your patient ca

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------






ABOUT THE ROLE

Counsel Health is seeking a high-caliber Enterprise Marketing Manager to help build and scale our marketing strategy and pipeline. This role sits at the intersection of demand generation, sales enablement, and thought leadership, and is designed for someone who thrives on translating complex healthcare technology into compelling narratives in a fast-paced environment.

You will partner closely with Sales, Clinical, AI, and Product teams to design and execute high-impact campaigns, develop thought leadership content, and create sales enablement materials that shape how Counsel communicates its value to enterprise buyers.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design and execute demand and lead generation campaigns across media partners and paid social (LinkedIn and Google), targeting enterprise buyers. Run structured A/B tests across messaging, creative, and audience segments to continuously improve performance.

 - Plan and execute in-person and virtual events across Counsel’s enterprise segments.

 - Identify performance gaps across channels and recommend optimizations to ensure outcomes align with internal KPIs.

 - Strengthen Counsel’s position as a leader in AI-enabled care through high-quality thought leadership, including white papers and blog content.

 - Partner closely with the commercial team to create sales enablement assets that support enterprise conversations, including outbound messaging, custom decks, and one-pagers.

 - Collaborate with marketing leadership and the commercial team to define account-based marketing strategy and target accounts.

 - Manage the marketing activity calendar, ensuring all initiatives align with organizational priorities.

 - Measure, analyze, and report on the performance of enterprise marketing initiatives to marketing and sales leadership.

 - Leverage AI tools to accelerate campaign development and create interactive experiences that support thought leadership and lead generation efforts.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5-7+ years of B2B marketing experience in healthcare, digital health, or enterprise SaaS, with 3+ years focused on demand and lead generation in a high-growth environment.

 - Proven experience planning and executing marketing programs across paid social, content syndication, events, webinars, and related channels.

 - Demonstrated track record of generating pipeline for a sales organization, with ownership of MQLs, SQLs, and net-new revenue. Able to clearly articulate how your work has driven revenue opportunities.

 - Comfortable using data to guide experimentation and decision-making, with the ability to evaluate performance across campaigns, messaging tests, and growth channels.

 - Strong ability to produce high-quality marketing and sales assets, including white papers and one-pagers.

 - Analytical mindset, using performance data to inform marketing decisions and develop hy

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Enterprise
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




ABOUT THE ROLE

We have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are scaling fast. We need a Senior Product Manager, Enterprise to own how Counsel's product shows up inside the world's leading healthcare organizations — and to build the consumer experiences that make those partnerships actually work. Your role is to not just make Counsel bigger one partnership at a time, but make Counsel better for everyone.

This role is equal parts consumer product and enterprise platform. You'll ship net-new features for patients — onboarding flows, evals, care interactions, clinical touchpoints — and you'll do it with a constraint that sharpens every decision: what you build has to work across all of our partners, not just the one asking. You will own our partner integration surface: the APIs, customization layers, and embedded experiences that let healthcare organizations bring Counsel's care capabilities into their products. You'll co-design with partners, build a product their consumers love, and build platform primitives that make each new integration easier than the last.

This role can be remote, with a strong preference for our NYC office.



What you’ll do

 - Co-design with partners: push back where the vision is wrong, push harder where it isn't going far enough

 - Build platform primitives that make each new integration easier than the last, so the team isn't rebuilding from scratch every time

 - Own partner relationships end to end, navigating executive conversations with clarity and earning trust through what you deliver

 - Champion a high bar for the end user experience in every integration, every conversation, every build



Requirements

 - 4+ years of product management experience with a meaningful focus on B2B2C products

 - Strong technical fluency: APIs, integrations, and platform architecture. You make the right calls and earn the respect of your engineering partners

 - Experience on a B2B2C product where the end user experience actually mattered for the success of the partnership, and where the business had strong opinions about how that experience should look

 - A systems thinker: you design for the current partner and build for the next five

 - The kind of relationship with partners and engineering where they actually want your opinion, not just your roadmap update

 - A genuine obsession with the end user experience: you've pushed back on lazy integrations, raised the bar on what good looks like, and built things that made a real difference



You're a great fit if

 - You've worked on platform and API-first products where your product lived inside someone else's brand

 - You're deeply interested in AI and where it's going, and you want to work somewhere that's building something real with it

 - You've built embedded or white-label product experiences

 - You actively use AI tools in your product wo

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer (Backend)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------






ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Senior Backend Engineer to help define the future of AI-powered, human-led healthcare. You'll sit on our Platform & Integrations team, owning the core product primitives the rest of the company builds on: external APIs, health data models, retrieval infrastructure, and enterprise integrations.

This role is central to scaling our platform: supporting enterprise healthcare clients, powering AI-driven clinical workflows, and keeping distributed systems correct and reliable. Much of this is greenfield, especially how we model and operationalize longitudinal health data from wearables and external systems.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and evolve our external API and embedded product layer with long-term stability, disciplined versioning, and enterprise-grade craftsmanship

 - Evolve our agentic retrieval harness to support patient and clinical search across structured and unstructured data, with strict permission and audit guarantees

 - Build reusable platform primitives that let external partners embed and extend our AI capabilities, so every integration adds lasting platform leverage

 - Contribute to core data models that unify structured health records, unstructured inputs, and high-frequency time-series signals



WHO YOU ARE

 - Cares deeply about API craftsmanship and long-term system design

 - Has built complex systems that external customers rely on

 - Enjoys modeling messy real-world data, especially high-frequency signals, into clean, durable abstractions

 - Has built comprehensive search or RAG systems at production scale

 - Has helped scale early systems from 1 to 10 in a fast-paced environment

 - Thrives in small, high-leverage teams where ownership is real

 - Has a track record of maintaining and leveling up operational excellence across engineering organizations
   


WHAT YOU BRING

 - 5+ years building production backend systems, ideally in high-growth environments

 - Experience designing and maintaining external-facing APIs used by customers or partners

 - Strong data modeling instincts, particularly around time-series or high-frequency data

 - Experience working with distributed systems and multi-tenant architectures

 - Comfort contributing to greenfield architectural design while shipping pragmatically




ROLE LOCATION:

NYC - hybrid, Boston - hybrid, or East Coast - remote




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Impact: Be at the forefront and contribute to groundbreaking, AI-driven healthcare experiences, directly interface with execs at the world's leading tech & healthcare orgs

 - Growth: Significant opportunities for broad ownership and professional development within our rapidly scaling startup

 - Compensation: Competitive salary + equity

 - Benefits: Comprehensive healthcare, vision + dental, 401K, Unlimited PTO, Parental Leave

----------------------------------------------------

source_jobs.title: Analytics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------






ABOUT THE ROLE

We've built a strong data foundation: a cloud warehouse, ETL pipelines, and event tracking are all in place. Now we need someone to make that data mean something.

We're looking for an Analytics Engineer to own the semantic layer, building and maintaining the core data models the rest of the company relies on to turn raw data into insight. You'll be the person everyone trusts to answer "how do we measure this?"

You'll be our first dedicated analytics hire, which means you set the standards, choose the patterns, and shape how Counsel uses data for years to come. The infrastructure is built, the warehouse is running, and the company is growing. What's missing is the person who makes the data layer actually work, turning raw tables into the trusted models that power every dashboard, every data product, and every strategic decision.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and maintain the data models that define our core business metrics, from clinical outcomes to revenue to utilization, and own the sources of truth the whole company relies on

 - Run dbt orchestration with integrated alerting, CI/CD pipelines, and data quality testing that catches issues before they reach a dashboard

 - Stand up reverse ETL pipelines that feed clean, modeled data back into production systems, including population cohort definitions, marketing intelligence, and operational features

 - Implement and maintain access controls for PHI, keeping the data layer HIPAA-compliant while staying accessible to the people who need it




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 3+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data analytics, or a hybrid data role

 - Deep expertise in SQL and dbt

 - Experience with BigQuery or comparable cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, Redshift)

 - Experience working in a regulated industry (healthcare, fintech, insurance, government) with a proven ability to manage sensitive data and access controls, HIPAA familiarity a plus

 - Experience with data orchestration tooling (Paradigm, dbt Cloud, Airflow, or similar) and integrating analytics data back into production systems




WHO YOU ARE

 - Thinks in business entities and stakeholder questions, not just tables and joins, and turns fuzzy requirements into clean, well-modeled data

 - Treats dbt as a primary tool, writing clean, modular, well-tested transformations, with strong opinions about how a dbt project should be organized

 - Comfortable managing orchestration, environments, and reverse ETL pipelines without hand-holding, even without being a dedicated data engineer

 - Doesn't just model data, but asks why someone needs it and how it drives a patient or business outcome

 - Feels energized by creating structure in ambiguous, fast-moving environments, and can synthesize needs from multiple stakeholders into a clear data strategy




ROLE LOCATION:

NYC - hybrid, Boston - hybrid, or East

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer (Fullstack)

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a fullstack software engineer to help define the future of AI-powered, human-led healthcare. This is a hybrid role based out of our New York City (Flatiron) office, with a Boston office opening soon.

You'll carry real ownership over our product, with the chance to shape our engineering org as it grows. You'll work cross-functionally with product, clinical, and commercial teams, and interface directly with prominent healthcare organizations nationwide. Our engineering work spans full-stack web development, data infrastructure, mobile development, and AI/MLOps.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Work closely with Product and Design to ship and iterate on unique product experiences in AI-enabled care that improve patient engagement and outcomes

 - Work closely with our ML research team to bring cutting-edge AI/LLM techniques into clinical care and ship them in production

 - Improve our physician-facing care platform and integrate AI tooling that increases physician efficiency and clinical capability

 - Build robust integrations with large enterprise clients, including EMR integrations with healthcare providers, claims data structuring with health plans, and HIE records parsing




WHO YOU ARE

 - A self-motivated, creative doer who takes ownership of broad initiatives and drives them to completion, navigating ambiguity with ease

 - Has hands-on experience building consumer-facing products from the ground up in a high-growth startup environment

 - Thrives working alongside exceptional teammates and draws energy from fast-moving, collaborative environments

 - Excited about working at the frontier of medical AI




WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - 2+ years of professional experience, ideally at a startup or fast-moving company

 - Proficiency with our stack: React (TypeScript), Next.js, React Native, Postgres (Supabase), HTML/CSS (Tailwind), and AWS infrastructure

 - Experience building at scale, serving hundreds of thousands of end users and large enterprise clients

 - Opinionated about leveling up your own engineering skills and the team around you




ROLE LOCATION

NYC - hybrid, Boston - hybrid




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Impact: Be at the forefront and contribute to groundbreaking, AI-driven healthcare experiences, directly interface with execs at the world's leading tech & healthcare orgs

 - Growth: Significant opportunities for broad ownership and professional development within our rapidly scaling startup

 - Compensation: Competitive salary + equity

 - Benefits: Comprehensive healthcare, vision + dental, 401K, Unlimited PTO, Parental Leave

-----------------------------------------------------


OUR VALUES

 - Patients come first.
   We are clinicians 1st, technologists 2nd. We act with patient trust/safety at the center of every decision.

 - Innovate with optimism.
   We are optimistic as we build the future of care de

We're looking for a Senior Software Engineer to help define the future of AI-powered, human-led healthcare. This role can be remote, or hybrid for those based in NY/Boston or able to relocate to our in-person office.

You'll carry real ownership over our product, with the chance to shape our engineering org as it grows. You'll work cross-functionally with product, clinical, and commercial teams, and interface directly with prominent healthcare organizations nationwide. Our engineering work spans full-stack web development, data infrastructure, mobile development, and AI/MLOps.

 - Improve our physician-facing care platform and integrate AI tooling that increases physician efficiency and clinical capability

 - Architect scalable backend systems, such as health data ingestion pipelines and enterprise client integrations
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - An owner of broad initiatives, driving them to completion, and navigating ambiguity with ease

 - Hands-on experience building consumer-facing products from the ground up in a high-growth startup environment

 - Thrives working alongside exceptional teammates and draws energy from fast-moving, collaborative environments

 - Excited about working at the frontier of medical AI
   
   

What You'll Bring

 - Proficiency with our stack: React, Next.js, Node.js (TypeScript), React Native, Postgres, AWS, Terraform, and Redis

What We Offer

 - Growth: Professional development and leadership opportunities within a rapidly expanding tech-enabled care delivery organization.

 - Exposure: Gain hands-on experience in scaling a fast-growing healthcare technology company and directly interface with executives at the world’s leading healthcare organizations.

source_jobs.title: Head of Legal
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




ABOUT THE ROLE

At Counsel Health, we’re building in a space where thoughtful legal guidance is core to how we grow. We’re hiring our founding Head of Legal to help shape the company at an important stage, when strong legal and compliance leadership can unlock growth, protect the business, and make it easier for teams to move quickly with confidence.

This is a foundational role. You’ll work across commercial, corporate, and healthcare regulatory matters, and you’ll help build the legal function from the ground up. You’ll partner closely with leaders across the company to support new commercial deals, navigate the regulatory landscape, and establish the compliance systems we need to scale responsibly.

Reporting to the SVP of Finance & Operations, we’re looking for someone who brings sound judgment, a practical commercial mindset, and a genuine excitement for helping an ambitious company grow in a complex regulatory environment. The right person will know how to balance risk with momentum and will be motivated by building the legal backbone of a company with meaningful impact.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Partner with teams across the company on a wide range of legal questions, helping the business move quickly and thoughtfully

 - Lead commercial legal work, including customer, vendor, and partner agreements

 - Build scalable legal and compliance processes that support growth without creating unnecessary friction

 - Own key healthcare regulatory and compliance areas

 - Help design the legal and compliance infrastructure behind our insurance billing operations

 - Support privacy and information security work tied to customer needs, diligence processes, and future financings

 - Support equity and debt financings and help manage broader corporate matters

 - Oversee ongoing work across employment, IP, governance, tax, and other core legal responsibilities

 - Help define what a great in-house legal function looks like at Counsel Health





WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Experience early in your career at a top-tier law firm, ideally in a corporate practice

 - Deep experience in healthcare, either as counsel to healthtech companies or in-house at a healthcare or healthtech business

 - A practical, business-minded approach to legal work

 - Strong judgment and the ability to balance risk, speed, and company priorities

 - Comfort working independently and owning complex legal matters from start to finish

 - Experience managing outside counsel and driving important workstreams forward without close oversight

 - Excitement about building systems, processes, and a function from the ground up

 


BONUS POINTS FOR:

 - Experience being an early legal hire at a startup or scaling company

 - Familiarity with insurance billing, healthcare regulatory frameworks, and approval processes

 - Experience helping a company grow through new markets, products,

source_jobs.title: Talent Partner (Engineering & Product) - 6 month contract to perm
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT COUNSEL http://counsel.health/

Counsel’s mission is to multiply the world's healthcare capacity. We believe in a future in which every person has a personalized, AI-enabled doctor always in their pocket. To accomplish this, we’re building the first fully-vertical, AI-native medical group from the ground up. We’ve redesigned the entire clinical stack with custom AI tools that make our in-house physicians 10x faster and smarter. [learn more on our blog https://www.counselhealth.com/blog]

Since our founding in 2023, we have formed partnerships with leading healthcare organizations and are on track to serve hundreds of thousands of patients on our platform within the next year. We have raised over $35M from a16z, Google Ventures, Pear VC and other top-tier VC firms.

Our team combines deep expertise in medicine (Harvard, Mt. Sinai, Mass General, UCSF) and technology (MIT, Google, DeepMind, NVIDIA, Stripe, Meta) — and we’re looking for exceptional people to join us as we scale.


Join us as we build the future of AI in healthcare!

-----------------------------------------------------




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Talent Partner who lives and breathes Engineering hiring. You know the difference between a great engineer and a good one, and you care deeply about getting that call right. You bring that same rigor to Product, with enough depth to recognize strong product thinking when you see it.

This is a role for someone who moves fast and holds a high bar at the same time. You've built pipelines, closed hard searches, and pushed back on mediocre hires. You believe talent density is a strategy, and you want to be somewhere the stakes are real.

You're genuinely excited by what Counsel is building at the intersection of AI and healthcare. That excitement makes you a better partner to hiring managers, a better evaluator of candidates, and more invested in getting every hire right.

This is a full-time, 6-month contract with a path to permanent, hybrid out of Boston or NYC (Flatiron).

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own hiring across Engineering and Product, from first sourcing touch to signed offer, with support from senior team members on the harder calls

 - Partner with hiring managers to understand what great looks like for each role and keep pipelines moving

 - Source candidates directly instead of waiting on inbound

 - Own Ashby data integrity for every requisition you touch

 - Take feedback on calibration and level up your evaluation skills fast

 


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3-5 years of full-cycle recruiting experience, with meaningful time in technical roles

 - Working knowledge of engineering skill sets across backend, frontend, full-stack, ML/AI, data, and DevOps, beyond keyword matching

 - Some exposure to Product recruiting, or a clear hunger to build that muscle

 - Comfortable in an ATS (Ashby experience a plus, not required)

 - A track record of sourcing outbound, not just running process on inbound

 


YOU'RE A GREAT IF

 - You're curious by nature and genuinely into AI, building with it and forming real opinions on your own time

 - You want to help define what Talent Architecture looks like when a TA function is AI-native from day one, not bolted on later

 


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Impact: Ownership from week one, not a training wheels role

 - Growth: A path to permanent based on real performance

 - Exposure: Direct access to leadership at a company building at the edge of AI and healthcare

 - Compensation: Competitive contract rate, with equity on conversion to permanent

-----------------------------------------------------


OUR VALUES

 - Patients come first.
   We are clinicians 1st, technologists 2nd. We act with patient trust/safety at the center of every decision.

 - Innovate with optimism.
   We are optimistic as we build the future of care delivery from first principles. Challenge the status quo when needed, no sacred cows.

 - Right answer wins.
   Debate openly and pick the strongest path. We op

notes: Healthcare-focused startup with AI-native medical group, strong engineering presence, and rapid hiring pace.
